Title: The Help body markup
Summary: Tight markdown subset rendered to themed components by a hand rolled parser.
Body:
## Why a subset

Help bodies want pixel chrome around code blocks, callouts, and keyboard chips. Full markdown plus a custom plugin layer is more code than just rolling a parser that emits the right components from the start. The renderer at `client/src/features/help/render.tsx` is about 250 lines.

## Supported syntax

```
## h2 heading
### h3 heading

Plain paragraph. Lines wrap.

- bullet
- another

1. ordered
2. ordered

` + inline code + `

triple backtick fenced
code block (no language tag yet)
triple backtick close

> note: a neutral callout
> tip: an emerald callout for happy paths
> warn: an amber callout for gotchas

[Internal link](/dialogs)
[External link](https://example.com)

:kbd[Ctrl+K] keyboard chip
```

## What it does NOT support

No emphasis (`*italic*` or `**bold**`) on purpose. Bleepforge's typography choices already make headings and code visually distinct enough; emphasis inside body prose tends to look noisy in pixel fonts.

No tables. If you need one, fall back to a bullet list of "X: Y" pairs.

No images. Help is text. If you must reference an image, link to its asset gallery preview instead.

## Where to author

Help content is authored by editing the JSON files under `data/help/<categoryId>/` directly. The schema is in [shared/src/help.ts](shared/src/help.ts); the renderer is in [client/src/features/help/render.tsx](client/src/features/help/render.tsx). Use the renderer's source as your reference for what the markup will look like on the page.
